executive_summary: The system allows users to create an account using an email and password, with the requirement that the email must be unique and verified before the first login. Password length must be between 8 and 12 characters. No additional password complexity rules or email verification details have been specified.
functional_summary: Users can register by providing an email and password. After account creation, the user must verify their email address before they are allowed to log in for the first time. The email address used for registration must be unique across all accounts.
open_questions: What are the password complexity requirements (e.g., uppercase, special characters)?
How is email verification sent (e.g., link, code)?
Does the email verification link or code have an expiration time?
What error handling and user feedback should be provided (e.g., for duplicate email, invalid password)?
What is the account creation flow (e.g., confirmation page, duplicate email handling)?
assumptions: The password is accepted as plain text during registration.
Email verification is required only once before the first login.
The system will handle email uniqueness validation at the time of account creation.
risks: Lack of password complexity requirements may lead to weak passwords and security vulnerabilities.
Undefined email verification mechanism could cause integration delays or user confusion.
Missing error handling specifications may result in poor user experience or incomplete implementation.
